stages.name: explore
stages.description: Breadth-first link discovery and crawling

stages.name: join
stages.description: Follow links for each row using HTTP (wget)

stages.name: wgetJoin
stages.description: Follow links using the wgetJoin primitive

stages.name: wgetExplore
stages.description: Breadth-first crawling using the wgetExplore primitive

stages.name: visit
stages.description: Fetch a URL from a column using browser automation (JavaScript-rendered)

stages.name: wget
stages.description: Fetch a URL from a column using HTTP

stages.name: visitJoin
stages.description: Follow links using browser automation for each row

stages.name: visitExplore
stages.description: Breadth-first crawling using browser automation

stages.name: extract
stages.description: Extract fields using selector-map configuration

args.name: extractors
args.description: List of {selector, method, as} extractor maps

stages.name: flatSelect
stages.description: Segment HTML block and create one row per segment (requires example-plugin)

args.name: extractors
args.description: Extractor maps applied inside each segment

stages.example:
- stage: flatSelect
  args:
    - "div.product"
    - - { selector: "h3", method: "text", as: "name" }

stages.name: intelligent_explore
stages.description: Like explore, but uses LLM to infer selector from natural language prompt

args.name: prompt
args.description: Natural language description of target links

stages.name: intelligent_join
stages.description: Join with LLM-inferred selector and actions

stages.name: intelligent_wget_explore
stages.description: Like intelligent_explore but uses HTTP-only (Wget) fetching instead of the browser. For static SERPs where the LLM doesn't need a JS-rendered DOM.

args.name: prompt
args.description: Natural language description of target links

args.name: maxLinks
args.description: Cap on links per parent row

stages.example:
- stage: intelligent_wget_explore
  args: ["next page link at the bottom of the catalogue", 1, 10]

stages.name: intelligent_wget_join
stages.description: Like intelligent_join but uses HTTP-only (Wget) fetching for detail pages instead of the browser. Big cost win on static detail pages.
stages.example:
- stage: intelligent_wget_join
  args: ["the link to each book detail page", 10]

stages.name: iextract
stages.description: LLM extraction on HTML block with chunking, produces multiple columns
stages.example:
- stage: iextract
  args: ["Extract product title, price and sku", "prod_"]

stages.name: intelligent_flatSelect
stages.description: Segmentation and multi-row extraction with LLM-inferred selector (uses template caching + RoadRunner)
stages.example:
- stage: intelligent_flatSelect
  args: ["product cards", "extract name and price", "product"]

stages.name: intelligentExtract
stages.description: LLM-powered ad-hoc extraction from an HTML field (row transform)
stages.example:
- stage: intelligentExtract
  args: ["description_html", "Extract brand and model", "llm_json"]

stages.name: intelligent_table
stages.description: Placeholder stage for LLM table extraction (currently no-op)

stages.name: echo
stages.description: Debug helper; writes _echo column

stages.name: cache
stages.description: Persist the current dataset in Spark memory (equivalent to .cache())

stages.name: store
stages.description: Save the current dataset under a label for multi-source composition

args.name: label
args.description: Label to store the dataset under

stages.name: reset
stages.description: Start fresh with an empty dataset (for multi-source composition)

stages.name: union_with
stages.description: Union the current dataset with one or more stored labels

args.name: labels
args.description: One or more stored dataset labels to union

stages.name: dedup
stages.description: Deduplicate rows. With no args: full-row distinct. With args: deduplicate by key columns

stages.name: filter_country
stages.description: Keep only rows whose country field is in the allowed list

stages.name: sentiment
stages.description: LLM-based sentiment + named-entity analysis (calls Groq when GROQ_API_KEY is present, else a small IT/EN lexicon fallback). Adds EXACTLY these columns: `sentiment` (numeric score), `polarity` (positive|negative|neutral), `entities`, `count` (=1). It does NOT create <field>_sentiment_score or <field>_sentiment_label — referencing those fails with UNRESOLVED_COLUMN.

stages.name: aggregatesentiment
stages.description: Reduce-by-key aggregation; sums sentiment and count by key

stages.name: avg_sentiment_by_key
stages.description: Group-by-key and compute average sentiment into avg_sentiment

stages.name: sentiment_monthly
stages.description: Macro-stage: runs sentiment, derives month from date, then aggregates by month

stages.name: sum_sales
stages.description: Reduce-by-key aggregation; sums numeric sales by country

stages.name: load_csv
stages.description: Load a CSV file into the pipeline (creates new dataset)

args.name: pathOrOptions
args.description: Path string or {path, header, inferSchema, ...} map

stages.name: save_csv
stages.description: Save current dataset as CSV and continue pipeline

stages.name: load_union
stages.description: Load multiple datasets and union them by column name (allowMissingColumns=true)

stages.name: unionByName
stages.description: Union the current dataset with external datasets loaded from specs

stages.name: load_avro
stages.description: Load an Avro file into the pipeline

stages.name: load_delta
stages.description: Load a Delta Lake table into the pipeline

stages.name: load_iceberg
stages.description: Load an Apache Iceberg table into the pipeline

stages.name: load_bigquery
stages.description: Load a BigQuery table into the pipeline

stages.name: load_mongodb
stages.description: Load a MongoDB collection into the pipeline

stages.name: load_kafka
stages.description: Load from a Kafka topic into the pipeline

stages.name: searchEngine
stages.description: Search by EAN using a provider (Google Custom Search / Bing) and optionally enrich results

args.name: config
args.description: {provider, ean, num_results, image_search, enrich, api_key, cx, as}

stages.example:
- stage: searchEngine
  args:
    - provider: "google"
      ean: "$ean"
      num_results: 10

stages.name: socialAPI
stages.description: Fetch JSON from social/financial APIs with auth via header or query param

args.name: config
args.description: {provider, endpoint, params, auth_token, method, body, as}

stages.example:
- stage: socialAPI
  args:
    - provider: "twitter"
      endpoint: "tweets/search/recent"
      auth_token: "${TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN}"
      as: "tweets_json"

stages.name: eurostatAPI
stages.description: Fetch Eurostat REST API JSON

stages.name: istatAPI
stages.description: Fetch ISTAT SDMX JSON

stages.name: oecdAPI
stages.description: Fetch OECD SDMX-JSON statistics (international macro). Public, no API key.

stages.name: worldBankAPI
stages.description: Fetch World Bank Open Data indicators. Public, no API key. PARTITIONED: driver probe on page=1 reads meta.pages, then fans out 1 Spookystuff trace per page across executors (HTTP equivalent of Spark JDBC partitionColumn + numPartitions).

args.name: config
args.description: {country (ISO-3 or all), indicator, per_page (default 1000, max 32500), numPartitions (optional cap), params: {date: '1960:2024'}, as}

stages.name: fredAPI
stages.description: Fetch FRED (St. Louis Fed) US macro series. Requires FRED_API_KEY or cloud_credentials provider=fred.

stages.name: bancaItaliaAPI
stages.description: Fetch Banca d'Italia Infostat SDMX 2.1. Public, no API key.

args.name: config
args.description: {flow, key, params: {startPeriod, endPeriod, format: jsondata}, as}

stages.name: overpassAPI
stages.description: OpenStreetMap Overpass QL query, JSON output. Public, rate-limited ~10k queries/day per IP.

stages.name: enrichMatchingScore
stages.description: Recalculate matching score based on input fields and iextract output prefix

stages.name: imageSimilarity
stages.description: Evaluate image similarity scores using LLM + heuristics

stages.name: priceNormalize
stages.description: Normalize price strings into price_numeric, price_currency, and price_<currency>

stages.name: oddsNormalize
stages.description: Convert odds to odds_decimal

stages.name: propertyNormalize
stages.description: Compute area_sqm and price_per_sqm

stages.name: realEstateArbitrage
stages.description: Compute simple z-score outliers (arbitrage_z, is_arbitrage) from price_per_sqm

stages.name: propertyCluster
stages.description: Cluster real-estate listings using unsupervised learning (KMeans/DBSCAN). Produces cluster_id

stages.name: propertyClusterML
stages.description: Cluster using KMeans or GMM (ML variant)

stages.name: python_row_transform
stages.description: Apply a registered Python row transform function. Use python_row_transform:<function_name> syntax

stages.name: technicalIndicators
stages.description: Compute technical indicators for financial time series

stages.name: fundamentalAnalysis
stages.description: Compute fundamental analysis metrics

stages.name: portfolioDataPrep
stages.description: Prepare data for portfolio analysis

stages.name: surebetFinder
stages.description: Find arbitrage opportunities in betting odds data

stages.name: sql_query
stages.description: Execute an arbitrary Spark SQL query against the current dataset (registered as temp view 'df'). Returns the query result as the new pipeline dataset.

args.name: query
args.description: Spark SQL query. The inbound dataset is available as table 'df'. All columns are StringType.

stages.example:
- stage: sql_query
  args: ["SELECT era, COUNT(*) AS count FROM df GROUP BY era ORDER BY count DESC"]

stages.name: rag_ingest
stages.description: Bulk-upsert the current DataFrame into a RAG index (LlamaCloud or Weaviate). Credentials are taken from cloud_credentials for the org. Passthrough for the upstream DataFrame.

args.name: config
args.description: { provider: llamacloud|weaviate, index_name: <name>, text_column: <col>, id_column?: <col>, metadata_columns?: csv|list, batch_size?: int }

stages.example:
- stage: rag_ingest
  args:
    - provider: llamacloud
      index_name: webrobot_kb
      project_name: webrobot
      text_column: content
      id_column: url
      metadata_columns: title,author,scraped_at,source_url
      batch_size: 100

stages.name: rag_query
stages.description: For each row, run a RAG semantic search and append the top-k hits as a JSON column. Useful to enrich scraped rows with related knowledge-base entries.

args.name: config
args.description: { provider, index_name, question_column, top_k?, output_column?, filter_<k>?: <v>... }

stages.example:
- stage: rag_query
  args:
    - provider: llamacloud
      index_name: webrobot_kb
      question_column: article_summary
      top_k: 5
      output_column: similar_articles
      filter_category: news

stages.name: rag_dedup
stages.description: Drop (or tag) rows whose top-1 similarity against the RAG index exceeds a threshold. Used for re-scrape pipelines to skip already-ingested content.

args.name: config
args.description: { provider, index_name, text_column, similarity_threshold?, on_match?: drop|tag, match_column?: <col> }

stages.example:
- stage: rag_dedup
  args:
    - provider: llamacloud
      index_name: webrobot_kb
      text_column: content
      similarity_threshold: 0.92
      on_match: drop

stages.name: rag_index_stats
stages.description: Replace the current dataset with a single-row metrics snapshot of the RAG index. Useful as a final stage for index-health dashboards.
stages.example:
- stage: rag_index_stats
  args:
    - provider: llamacloud
      index_name: webrobot_kb

stages.name: load_parquet
stages.description: Load a Parquet file/dir as the current dataset (Spark-native columnar).

stages.name: save_parquet
stages.description: Write the current DataFrame as Parquet (passthrough sink).
stages.example:
- stage: save_parquet
  args:
    - path: s3a://wr-data/output/
      mode: overwrite
      partitionBy: ingest_date

stages.name: load_json
stages.description: Load JSONL or multi-line JSON as the current dataset.

stages.name: save_json
stages.description: Write the current DataFrame as JSON-lines.
stages.example:
- stage: save_json
  args:
    - path: s3a://wr-data/output/
      mode: append

stages.name: save_avro
stages.description: Write the current DataFrame as Avro.
stages.example:
- stage: save_avro
  args:
    - path: s3a://wr-data/output/events.avro
      mode: overwrite

stages.name: save_delta
stages.description: Write the current DataFrame as a Delta Lake table (ACID + time-travel via Delta tooling).
stages.example:
- stage: save_delta
  args:
    - path: s3a://wr-lakehouse/scraped
      mode: append

stages.name: save_bigquery
stages.description: Write the current DataFrame to a BigQuery table.
stages.example:
- stage: save_bigquery
  args:
    - table: my-project.analytics.scraped_events
      mode: append

stages.name: save_xml
stages.description: Write the current DataFrame as XML.
stages.example:
- stage: save_xml
  args:
    - path: s3a://wr-data/output/feed/
      rowTag: item

stages.name: save_mongodb
stages.description: Write the current DataFrame to a MongoDB collection.
stages.example:
- stage: save_mongodb
  args:
    - connection.uri: mongodb+srv://user:pass@cluster/db
      database: webrobot
      collection: leads

stages.name: save_cassandra
stages.description: Write the current DataFrame to a Cassandra table.
stages.example:
- stage: save_cassandra
  args:
    - keyspace: webrobot
      table: events

stages.name: save_elasticsearch
stages.description: Write the current DataFrame to an Elasticsearch index (`path` = index name).
stages.example:
- stage: save_elasticsearch
  args:
    - path: scraped_articles
      es.nodes: es.internal

stages.name: save_kafka
stages.description: Publish DataFrame rows to a Kafka topic (DF must include a `value` column).
stages.example:
- stage: save_kafka
  args:
    - kafka.bootstrap.servers: kafka:9092
      topic: scraped_events

stages.name: save_iceberg
stages.description: Write the current DataFrame as an Apache Iceberg table.
stages.example:
- stage: save_iceberg
  args:
    - path: local.db.scraped_events
      mode: append

stages.name: save_db
stages.description: Generic JDBC writer (any driver). Use save_postgres/save_mysql for DX wrappers.

args.name: config
args.description: { url?, dbtable, user?, password?, mode?, driver?, batchsize?, truncate? }

stages.example:
- stage: save_db
  args:
    - url: jdbc:postgresql://host:5432/db
      dbtable: public.events
      user: writer
      password: ${DB_PASSWORD}
      mode: append

stages.name: load_postgres
stages.description: Load a Postgres table (auto JDBC URL from host+port+database, defaults env fallback).

args.name: config
args.description: { host?, port?, database?, dbtable, user?, password?, partitionColumn?, lowerBound?, upperBound?, numPartitions?, fetchsize? }

stages.example:
- stage: load_postgres
  args:
    - host: my-pg.internal
      database: webrobotdb
      dbtable: public.scraped_rows

stages.name: save_postgres
stages.description: Write the current DataFrame to a Postgres table.

args.name: config
args.description: { host?, port?, database?, dbtable, user?, password?, mode?, batchsize?, truncate? }

stages.example:
- stage: save_postgres
  args:
    - host: my-pg.internal
      database: webrobotdb
      dbtable: public.leads
      mode: append

stages.name: load_mysql
stages.description: Load a MySQL table (auto JDBC URL from host+port+database, port defaults to 3306).

args.name: config
args.description: { host?, port?, database?, dbtable, user?, password?, ...partition tuning }

stages.example:
- stage: load_mysql
  args:
    - host: mysql.internal
      database: analytics
      dbtable: orders

stages.name: save_mysql
stages.description: Write the current DataFrame to a MySQL table.

args.name: config
args.description: { host?, port?, database?, dbtable, user?, password?, mode?, batchsize?, truncate? }

stages.example:
- stage: save_mysql
  args:
    - host: mysql.internal
      database: analytics
      dbtable: leads
      mode: append

actions.name: click
actions.description: Click on an element matching a CSS selector or natural-language description

params.name: target
params.description: CSS selector or natural language description of the element to click

examples: click on the first product link

actions.name: type
actions.description: Type text into an input field (clears the field first)

params.name: target
params.description: Target input element (selector or natural language)

examples: type 'Italy' in the country field then press Enter

actions.name: scroll
actions.description: Scroll the page or an element to trigger lazy-loading or pagination

params.name: amount
params.description: Pixels to scroll, or 'bottom' for full scroll (default: one viewport)

examples: scroll down to load more products
scroll to the bottom of the page

actions.name: wait
actions.description: Wait for an element to appear, a condition to be met, or a fixed time

params.name: target
params.description: CSS selector, natural language condition, or milliseconds

actions.name: select
actions.description: Select an option from a <select> dropdown element

params.name: value
params.description: Option value or visible text to select

examples: choose 'Highest price' from the sort by dropdown

actions.name: hover
actions.description: Hover the mouse over an element to trigger tooltip, menu or lazy-load
examples: hover on the main menu item to open submenu

actions.name: navigate
actions.description: Navigate the browser to a specific URL or relative path

params.name: url
params.description: Absolute URL, relative path, or $column reference

actions.name: submit
actions.description: Submit a form by pressing Enter or clicking the submit button

params.name: target
params.description: Form or submit button selector (optional — defaults to active form)

actions.name: eval
actions.description: Execute arbitrary JavaScript in the page context for advanced interactions

params.name: script
params.description: JavaScript expression or statement to execute in the browser

actions.name: none
actions.description: No action — use when the stage should navigate/collect without any prior interaction
